message: agent1: implementing click.edit() process_priority feature. Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent2 here: implementing escape_shell param for click.edit() in src/click/termui.py and src/click/_termui_impl.py. Will add optional escape_shell arg to edit() signature and use shlex.quote() in _termui_impl.py Editor class. Will ping if I touch anything else.

Ack - we're both touching Editor class in _termui_impl.py and edit() in termui.py. Let's each add our own param (mine: escape_shell, yours: process_priority) as independent kwargs threaded through edit() -> Editor.__init__ -> Editor.edit_files, so our diffs are additive and don't conflict. I'll do my edits now; will share exact signature I land on so you can merge around it.

agent1 done: added process_priority: str | None = None to click.edit() and Editor.__init__ (src/click/termui.py, src/click/_termui_impl.py). In Editor.edit_file I build popen_kwargs (creationflags on Windows) before Popen, and call os.setpriority(os.PRIO_PROCESS, c.pid, nice) after Popen on POSIX, wrapped in try/except. Shouldn't conflict with your escape_shell since I only touch the command string construction point minimally (f'{editor} "{filename}"' unchanged) - let me know if you change that same line so we can layer cleanly.

FYI: checked disk, your process_priority changes aren't present in my copy of the repo - looks like we each have isolated working directories, not a shared filesystem. No actual clobbering risk. I've finished escape_shell (adds escape_shell: bool=False to click.edit() and Editor.__init__, quotes editor+filename via shlex in Editor.edit_file) and written patch.txt. Good luck with process_priority.

agent1: finished and wrote patch.txt for process_priority feature (git diff already captured, only my changes present — you hadn't touched the files yet). Go ahead with your escape_shell edits now; my submission is locked in independently of further changes to the shared working tree.
